values.comment:
Convert this modules `QueryParm` to elm/url `QueryParameter` values
to use with Url.Builder.

values.name: resourceById
values.comment:
request : (Http.Request Http.Error Resource -> msg) -> Ginger.Id.ResourceId -> Cmd msg
    request toMsg id =
        Request.resourceById toMsg id
values.type: (Result.Result Http.Error (Ginger.Resource.ResourceWith Ginger.Resource.Edges) -> msg) -> Ginger.Id.ResourceId -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

values.name: resourceByName
values.comment:
request : (Http.Request Http.Error Resource -> msg) -> Cmd msg
    request toMsg =
        Request.resourceByName toMsg "home"
values.type: (Result.Result Http.Error (Ginger.Resource.ResourceWith Ginger.Resource.Edges) -> msg) -> String.String -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

values.name: resourceByPath
values.comment:
request : (Http.Request Http.Error Resource -> msg) -> Cmd msg
    request toMsg =
        Request.resourceByPath toMsg "/news"
values.type: (Result.Result Http.Error (Ginger.Resource.ResourceWith Ginger.Resource.Edges) -> msg) -> String.String -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

values.name: resourceQuery
values.comment:
request : Http.Request Http.Error (List Resource)
    request =
        Request.resourceQuery [ Text "amsterdam" ]
values.type: (Result.Result Http.Error (Ginger.Request.Results (Ginger.Resource.ResourceWith Ginger.Resource.Edges)) -> msg) -> List.List Ginger.Request.QueryParam -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

name: Ginger.Resource

aliases.name: Block
aliases.type: { body : Ginger.Translation.Translation, name : String.String, type_ : Ginger.Resource.BlockType, relatedRscId : Maybe.Maybe Ginger.Id.ResourceId, properties : Json.Decode.Value }

aliases.name: Edge
aliases.comment: A connection to a resource named by `Predicate`

aliases.name: Edges
aliases.comment:
The record we use to extend `ResourceWith a`.

You can render a list of resource depictions like so:

    viewDepictions : ResourceWith Edges -> List (Html msg)
    viewDepictions resource =
        List.map viewImage <|
            depictions Media.Medium resource

This next example won't compile because you need a `ResourceWith Edges`
and this signature indicates they are missing.

    viewDepictions : ResourceWith {} -> List (Html msg)
    viewDepictions resource =
        List.map viewImage <|
            depictions Media.Medium resource

aliases.name: ResourceWith
aliases.comment:
An Elm representation of a Ginger resource.

Note the `a` in the record definition, this an extensible record.
This means it includes _at least_ all of these fields but may have others
as well. This lets us reason about whether the edges are included in the data,
compile time. The Ginger REST API includes edges nested only one level deep,
but since the edges are also resources we can re-use this datatype like
`ResourceWith {}`. This tells use there are _no_ other fields besides the ones here.

So you'll see this used in function signatures like:

    ResourceWith Edges -- has edges

    ResourceWith {} -- does not have the edges

    ResourceWith a -- might have them but the code that's using this doesn't really care

_Note: the `ResourceWith {}` might actually have edges, they are just not fetched._
aliases.type: { a | id : Ginger.Id.ResourceId, title : Ginger.Translation.Translation, body : Ginger.Translation.Translation, subtitle : Ginger.Translation.Translation, summary : Ginger.Translation.Translation, path : String.String, category : List.NonEmpty.NonEmpty Ginger.Category.Category, properties : Json.Decode.Value, publicationDate : Maybe.Maybe Time.Posix, media : Ginger.Media.Media, blocks : List.List Ginger.Resource.Block }

values.name: category
values.comment:
The category of a `ResourceWith`.

Every resource has _one_ category, but can be part of a hierarchy of other
categories. For example `news` is part of `text > article > news`. This function
will always return the `Category` stored with the `ResourceWith`.

_Note: there hasn't been the need to expose any of the parent categories so far,
if there is please file an issue._

values.name: depiction
values.comment:
The image url of the `ResourceWith` depiction.

Returns the image url if there is a depiction _and_ the mediaclass exists.

values.name: depictions
values.comment:
The image urls of the `ResourceWith` depictions

Returns a list of image urls if there is a depiction _and_ the mediaclass exists.

values.name: edgesWithPredicate
values.comment:
Return all edges with a given predicate.

The returned resources won't have any edges themselves, indicated by the `{}`
in `ResourceWith {}`.

values.name: fromJsonWithEdges
values.comment: Decode a `ResourceWith` that has edges.

values.name: fromJsonWithoutEdges
values.comment: Decode a `ResourceWith` that does not have edges.

name: Ginger.Resource.Extra

aliases.name: AuthorName
aliases.type: { firstName : String.String, middleName : String.String, lastNamePrefix : String.String, lastName : String.String }

aliases.name: EventDate
aliases.type: { dateStart : Maybe.Maybe Time.Posix, dateEnd : Maybe.Maybe Time.Posix }

aliases.name: Location
aliases.type: { id : Ginger.Id.ResourceId, lat : Basics.Float, lng : Basics.Float }

name: Ginger.Translation

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Construct a Translation from a list of Language and String value pairs

values.name: html
values.comment: Translate and render as Html markup

values.name: isEmpty
values.comment: Checks if translated String is empty.

values.name: text
values.comment:
Translate and render as Html text

We try to unescape the escaped characters but if that fails we'll render the `Translation` as is.

Html elements will be filtered out and the text will be joined.

values.name: toIso639
values.comment: Convert a Language to an [Iso639](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-1_codes) String

values.name: toString
values.comment:
Get the translated String value.

_Unescapes character entity references, strips Html nodes and defaults to an empty String._

values.name: toStringEscaped
values.comment:
Get the _original_ translated String value as returned by the REST api.

_Defaults to an empty String._

values.name: withDefault
values.comment:
Get the translated String value.

The first argument is the fallback Language.

_Attempt fallback if translated value is missing, defaults to an empty String._

name: Ginger.Util

values.name: stripHtml
values.comment:
Remove all Html nodes from a String example :: String example = stripHtml "

Hola!

" --> "Hola!" _This unescapes html unicode characters as well. If parsing fails the original String is returned_

values.name: toHtml
values.comment:
Convert a String to Html

_This unescapes html unicode characters as well_

values.name: truncate
values.comment:
Truncate a String and append `...` if the String is longer than provided length


    example : String
    example =
        truncate 10 "Truncate a String and append `...` if the String is longer than provided length"

    --> "Truncate a..."

values.name: viewEither
values.comment:
If the boolean expression evaluates to `True`, render some html, otherwise,
render some other html.

    view : Model -> Html msg
    view model =
        , article []
            [ h1 [] [ text model.title ]
            , p []
                [ viewEither (model.category == Category.Article)
                    (\_ -> text "article")
                    (\_ -> text "something else")
                ]
            ]
values.type: Basics.Bool -> (() -> Html.Html msg) -> (() -> Html.Html msg) -> Html.Html msg

values.name: viewIf
values.comment:
Render some html if a boolean expression evaluates to `True`.

    view : Model -> Html msg
    view model =
        article []
            [ h1 [] [ text model.title ]
            , viewIf
                (model.category == Category.Article)
                (\_ -> p [] [ text "article" ])
            ]

values.name: viewIfNot
values.comment: Render some html if a boolean expression evaluates to `False`.

values.name: viewMaybe
values.comment:
Maybe, the resource has an author. Render what's in the `maybe`, or nothing.

    view : Model -> Html msg
    view model =
        article []
            [ h1 [] [ text "Article" ]
            , viewMaybe model.author
                (\authorName -> p [] [ text authorName ])
            ]
values.type: Maybe.Maybe a -> (a -> Html.Html msg) -> Html.Html msg

values.name: viewMaybeWithDefault
values.comment:
Render something else if there's nothing in the `maybe`.

    view : Model -> Html msg
    view model =
        article []
            [ h1 [] [ text "Article" ]
            , p []
                [ viewMaybeWithDefault model.author
                    (\_ -> text "Anonymous")
                    (\authorName -> text authorName)
                ]
            ]
values.type: Maybe.Maybe a -> (() -> Html.Html msg) -> (a -> Html.Html msg) -> Html.Html msg
